Well, the biggest thing that you're going to hear from every manufacturer is speeding up the transaction. When we look at it across all OEMs, number one thing that we hear is that the transactions take too long. When the customers are in the dealership, they go through the entire sales process and they enter into the negotiation experience. That time from the test drive till they walk out the door with their new vehicle, that's the most dangerous period of time for negative impact on customer satisfaction. What we know is that if the salespeople make more than two trips and the manager's not involved in the third trip, we are traditionally out of gross, out of time, and out of ethics. So when we look at what's happening, the salesperson making that initial presentation with confidence, being able to overcome their monetary objections, the salesperson bringing that presentation, that offer to purchase back up to the managers, and getting a secondary offer back to the customer. As we get closer and closer to transactional prices being 100% of our transactions instead of in some markets 20 and 30%, in a lot of our metros we're seeing 40, 50, 60% of those deals are transactional price already, meaning the customer has already been exposed to an online price that is deeply discounted before they even come in. As we get closer to 100% on that, doesn't matter what market you're in, there are very few things that you can do to maximize your profitability. Number one thing you can do to protect your gross is negotiate on their money and that's down payment.
